subject: A small step that helps your whole team
previewText: A 30-minute rhythm that helps teachers feel supported.
module-3-0-0: Start a Classroom Collaborative
module-4-0-0:
Hi {{ personalization_token('contact.firstname', 'fellow educator') }},

 

You have been doing something rare.

 

Over the last couple of weeks, you have created and launched multiple Spaces with students. That momentum does not happen by accident. It comes from strong teaching and a willingness to try something new.

 

If you are open to it, here is one small step that can make your team’s work feel lighter.

 

Start a local Classroom Collaborative

A Classroom Collaborative is a simple 30-minute meetup with a few colleagues. You can run it every week or every other week.

 

It is not another meeting to plan. It is a space to share what is working, swap ideas, and leave with something you can use tomorrow. A lot of teachers tell us the best part is this. They stop feeling like they have to figure it out alone.

 

What you will do

  • Invite 3, 6 colleagues
  • Pick a 30-minute time that repeats every week or every other week
  • Use a lightweight agenda to keep it practical

 

Why it matters

When one teacher finds a better way to plan, differentiate, or re-engage students, that win should not stay stuck in one classroom. You can help your colleagues get that time back. You do not need to be "the tech person" to do it.

 

If you have questions, hit reply. We are happy to help you get your first one scheduled.

 

- The SchoolAI team

 

P.S. If you already have a team meeting cadence, a Classroom Collaborative can plug right into that. The goal is momentum, not perfection.
